docs.title: AIDE
docs.text:
AIDE

Official Documentation

AIDE Project

https://github.com/aide/aide

1. Installation and Verification

Install AIDE

sudo dnf install -y aide
aide --version
2. Initialize Database

Create Baseline

sudo aide --init
sudo mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
3. Integrity Checking

Run Scan

sudo aide --check
4. Update Baseline

Create New Database

sudo aide --update
sudo mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
5. Troubleshooting

Verify Configuration

cat /etc/aide.conf
ls -l /var/lib/aide/
6. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Install AIDE.
  • Initialize database.
  • Detect file changes.
  • Update baseline.
  • Verify configuration.

docs.title: Ansible_Security
docs.text:
Ansible Security

Official Documentation

Ansible Documentation

https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/

RHEL System Roles

https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html/automating_system_administration_by_using_rhel_system_roles/

1. Installation

Install Ansible

sudo dnf install -y ansible-core
ansible --version
2. Inventory

Verify Inventory

cat inventory
ansible all -m ping
3. SELinux Automation

Apply SELinux Configuration

ansible-playbook selinux.yml
4. Firewalld Automation

Configure Firewall

ansible-playbook firewalld.yml
5. OpenSCAP Automation

Apply Remediation

ansible-playbook openscap-remediation.yml
6. Verification

Check Results

ansible all -m setup
7. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Configure inventory.
  • Execute playbooks.
  • Configure SELinux.
  • Configure Firewalld.
  • Apply OpenSCAP remediation.
  • Verify configuration.

docs.title: Auditd
docs.text:
Auditd

Official Documentation

Linux Audit Project

https://github.com/linux-audit/audit-documentation

audit-userspace

https://github.com/linux-audit/audit-userspace

1. Installation and Verification

Install and Verify Auditd

# Install Auditd
sudo dnf install -y audit

# Enable service
sudo systemctl enable --now auditd

# Verify service
systemctl status auditd

# Verify version
auditctl -v
2. Audit Rules

View Rules

# List active rules
sudo auditctl -l

# View persistent rules
sudo cat /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules

Create Rules

# Watch passwd file
sudo auditctl -w /etc/passwd -p wa -k passwd_changes

# Watch shadow file
sudo auditctl -w /etc/shadow -p wa -k shadow_changes

# Watch sudoers
sudo auditctl -w /etc/sudoers -p wa -k sudo_changes

Persistent Rules

# Create custom rules file
sudo vi /etc/audit/rules.d/custom.rules

# Reload Auditd
sudo augenrules --load
3. Searching Audit Logs

Search Events

# Search by key
sudo ausearch -k passwd_changes

# Search AVC denials
sudo ausearch -m AVC

# Search failed logins
sudo ausearch -m USER_LOGIN

Search by Time

# Today's events
sudo ausearch -ts today

# Recent events
sudo ausearch -ts recent
4. Reports

Generate Reports

# Authentication report
sudo aureport --auth

# Login report
sudo aureport --login

# File report
sudo aureport --file

# Summary report
sudo aureport --summary
5. Log Files

Audit Logs

# View audit log
sudo less /var/log/audit/audit.log

# Follow log
sudo tail -f /var/log/audit/audit.log
6. Troubleshooting

Verify Audit Configuration

# Verify service
systemctl status auditd

# View loaded rules
sudo auditctl -l

# Reload rules
sudo augenrules --load

# Restart Auditd
sudo systemctl restart auditd
7. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Install and enable Auditd.
  • Create audit watch rules.
  • Configure persistent audit rules.
  • Search audit events.
  • Generate audit reports.
  • Investigate authentication events.
  • Investigate SELinux AVC denials.
  • Verify Auditd configuration.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Authentication

Official Documentation

Red Hat Security Hardening Guide

https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html-single/security_hardening/

Linux-PAM

https://www.linux-pam.org/

SSSD Project

https://sssd.io/

1. PAM

Configuration

# View PAM configuration
ls /etc/pam.d/

# View system-auth
cat /etc/pam.d/system-auth

# View password-auth
cat /etc/pam.d/password-auth
2. SSSD

Installation

# Install SSSD
sudo dnf install -y sssd

# Enable service
sudo systemctl enable --now sssd

# Verify service
systemctl status sssd

Configuration

# View configuration
sudo cat /etc/sssd/sssd.conf

# Validate configuration
sudo sssctl config-check

# Restart service
sudo systemctl restart sssd
3. Identity Verification

User Lookup

# Query local and remote users
id username

getent passwd username

getent group

cat /etc/nsswitch.conf
4. Troubleshooting

Authentication Logs

journalctl -xe

journalctl -u sssd

sudo sss_cache -E
5. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Configure PAM.
  • Install and configure SSSD.
  • Verify user authentication.
  • Troubleshoot authentication failures.

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text:
Authorization

Official Documentation

Red Hat Security Hardening Guide

https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html-single/security_hardening/

1. sudo

Configuration

# Edit sudoers safely
sudo visudo

# Validate configuration
visudo -c

# View current privileges
sudo -l

sudoers.d

# List additional rules
ls -l /etc/sudoers.d/

# View rule file
sudo cat /etc/sudoers.d/*
2. Password Policies

Password Aging

# View password aging
chage -l username

# Maximum password age
sudo chage -M 90 username

# Minimum password age
sudo chage -m 7 username

# Warning days
sudo chage -W 14 username

System Defaults

# View login defaults
cat /etc/login.defs

# View password quality
cat /etc/security/pwquality.conf
3. Account Lockout

Failed Login Tracking

# Display failed logins
sudo faillock

# Reset failures
sudo faillock --user username --reset
4. Troubleshooting

Verify Permissions

# Verify sudo access
sudo -l

# Check login definitions
cat /etc/login.defs

# Check password policy
cat /etc/security/pwquality.conf
5. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Configure sudo privileges.
  • Validate sudoers configuration.
  • Configure password aging.
  • Configure password quality policies.
  • Reset failed login counters.
  • Troubleshoot authorization problems.

docs.title: Cryptography
docs.text:
Cryptography

Official Documentation

OpenSSL

https://docs.openssl.org/

GnuPG

https://gnupg.org/documentation/

1. OpenSSL

Version and Random Data

openssl version
openssl rand -hex 32

Hash Files

sha256sum file.txt
openssl dgst -sha256 file.txt
2. TLS Certificates

Generate Private Key

openssl genrsa -out server.key 4096

Generate Certificate Request

openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr

Generate Self-Signed Certificate

openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 \
-key server.key \
-out server.crt

Inspect Certificate

openssl x509 -in server.crt -text -noout
3. GPG

Generate Key

gpg --full-generate-key

List Keys

gpg --list-keys
gpg --list-secret-keys

Encrypt and Decrypt

gpg -e -r username file.txt
gpg -d file.txt.gpg

Sign and Verify

gpg --sign file.txt
gpg --verify file.txt.gpg
4. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Generate hashes.
  • Create TLS certificates.
  • Inspect certificates.
  • Generate GPG keys.
  • Encrypt and decrypt files.
  • Sign and verify files.

docs.title: Firewalld
docs.text:
Firewalld

Official Documentation

Red Hat Security Hardening Guide

https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html-single/security_hardening/

Firewalld Project

https://firewalld.org/

1. Installation and Verification

Install and Check Service

# Install Firewalld
sudo dnf install -y firewalld

# Enable service
sudo systemctl enable --now firewalld

# Verify service
systemctl status firewalld

# Verify firewall state
firewall-cmd --state
2. Zones

View Zones

firewall-cmd --get-default-zone
firewall-cmd --get-active-zones
firewall-cmd --get-zones
firewall-cmd --list-all

Change Default Zone

sudo firewall-cmd --set-default-zone=public
3. Services

View Services

firewall-cmd --list-services
firewall-cmd --get-services

Allow Services

sudo firewall-cmd --add-service=http
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=https
sudo firewall-cmd --reload

Remove Services

sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --remove-service=http
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
4. Ports

Open Ports

sudo firewall-cmd --add-port=8080/tcp
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-port=8443/tcp
sudo firewall-cmd --reload

Close Ports

sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --remove-port=8080/tcp
sudo firewall-cmd --reload

View Ports

firewall-cmd --list-ports
5. Rich Rules

Create Rich Rule

sudo firewall-cmd \
--permanent \
--add-rich-rule='rule family="ipv4" source address="192.168.1.0/24" service name="ssh" accept"

sudo firewall-cmd --reload

List Rich Rules

firewall-cmd --list-rich-rules
6. Runtime vs Permanent

Runtime Configuration

sudo firewall-cmd --add-service=http
firewall-cmd --list-services

Permanent Configuration

sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=http
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
firewall-cmd --list-services
7. Troubleshooting

Verify Configuration

firewall-cmd --check-config
firewall-cmd --list-all
firewall-cmd --list-all-zones

Service Status

systemctl status firewalld
journalctl -u firewalld
8. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Configure default zone.
  • Enable and disable services.
  • Open and close ports.
  • Configure rich rules.
  • Distinguish runtime and permanent configuration.
  • Reload firewall configuration.
  • Verify firewall rules.

docs.title: OpenSCAP
docs.text:
OpenSCAP

Official Documentation

OpenSCAP Project

https://www.open-scap.org/

Red Hat Security Hardening Guide

https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html-single/security_hardening/

SCAP Security Guide (SSG)

https://www.open-scap.org/security-policies/scap-security-guide/

1. Installation and Verification

Install OpenSCAP Tools

# Install OpenSCAP scanner and security content
sudo dnf install -y openscap-scanner scap-security-guide

# Verify installation
oscap --version
2. Security Content

Locate SCAP Content

# List installed SCAP content
ls -l /usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/

# Display information about the RHEL data stream
oscap info /usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml

List Available Profiles

# Display available compliance profiles
oscap info /usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml | less
3. Compliance Scanning

Run Security Scan

# Evaluate system compliance
sudo oscap xccdf eval \
--profile xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_profile_cis \
--results results.xml \
--report report.html \
/usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml

# Open HTML report
firefox report.html
4. Remediation

Generate Remediation Script

# Generate Bash remediation script
sudo oscap xccdf generate fix \
--profile xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_profile_cis \
--fix-type bash \
--output remediation.sh \
/usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml

# Apply remediation
sudo bash remediation.sh
5. Verification

Verify Compliance Again

# Run compliance scan after remediation
sudo oscap xccdf eval \
--profile xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_profile_cis \
--results after-remediation.xml \
--report after-remediation.html \
/usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml
6. Troubleshooting

Review Logs

# View system log
journalctl -xe

# Search SELinux AVC denials
ausearch -m AVC

# Analyze SELinux alerts
sealert -a /var/log/audit/audit.log
7. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Install OpenSCAP and SCAP Security Guide.
  • Locate installed SCAP content.
  • Identify available compliance profiles.
  • Run XCCDF compliance scans.
  • Generate HTML compliance reports.
  • Generate Bash remediation scripts.
  • Apply automated remediations.
  • Verify system compliance after remediation.
  • Troubleshoot scan failures and SELinux denials.

docs.title: SELinux
docs.text:
SELinux

Official Documentation

Red Hat Security Hardening Guide

https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html-single/security_hardening/

SELinux Project Wiki

https://github.com/SELinuxProject/selinux/wiki

1. Installation and Verification

Check SELinux Status

# Check current mode
getenforce
# Display detailed status
sestatus

# Display configuration
cat /etc/selinux/config
2. SELinux Modes

Temporary Mode Changes

# Switch to Permissive mode
sudo setenforce 0
# Switch to Enforcing mode
sudo setenforce 1

Permanent Mode Changes

# Edit configuration
sudo vi /etc/selinux/config
3. Security Contexts

File Contexts

# View file contexts
ls -Z
ls -lZ /var/www

Process Contexts

# View process contexts
ps -eZ
ps -eZ | grep httpd
4. File Labeling

Restore Default Labels

sudo restorecon -Rv /var/www

Temporary Labels

sudo chcon -t httpd_sys_content_t index.html

Persistent Labels

sudo semanage fcontext \
-a \
-t httpd_sys_content_t \
"/web(/.*)?"
sudo restorecon -Rv /web
sudo semanage fcontext -l
5. SELinux Booleans

View Booleans

getsebool -a
getsebool -a | grep http

Modify Booleans

sudo setsebool -P httpd_can_network_connect on
getsebool httpd_can_network_connect
6. SELinux Ports

View Ports

sudo semanage port -l

Add Port

sudo semanage port \
-a \
-t http_port_t \
-p tcp 8080

Remove Port

sudo semanage port \
-d \
-t http_port_t \
-p tcp 8080
7. Troubleshooting

Audit Logs

sudo ausearch -m AVC
sudo sealert -a /var/log/audit/audit.log
sudo less /var/log/audit/audit.log

Common Recovery

sudo restorecon -Rv /var/www
ls -lZ /var/www
getsebool -a
8. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Switch between Enforcing and Permissive mode.
  • Restore incorrect file labels.
  • Configure persistent file contexts.
  • Enable SELinux booleans.
  • Configure SELinux port mappings.
  • Investigate AVC denials.
  • Fix applications without disabling SELinux.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Troubleshooting

Official Documentation

Red Hat Documentation

https://docs.redhat.com/

1. SELinux

Investigate AVC Denials

ausearch -m AVC
sealert -a /var/log/audit/audit.log
restorecon -Rv /var/www
2. Firewalld

Verify Firewall

firewall-cmd --state
firewall-cmd --list-all
firewall-cmd --list-all-zones
3. Authentication

Verify Authentication

systemctl status sssd
id username
getent passwd username
4. Authorization

Verify sudo

sudo -l
visudo -c
5. Auditd

Search Audit Events

auditctl -l
ausearch -m AVC
aureport --summary
6. OpenSCAP

Verify Compliance

oscap --version
oscap info /usr/share/xml/scap/ssg/content/ssg-rhel9-ds.xml
7. AIDE

Verify Integrity

aide --check
8. EX415 Practice

Common Exam Tasks

  • Diagnose SELinux issues.
  • Diagnose firewall problems.
  • Troubleshoot authentication.
  • Troubleshoot sudo.
  • Investigate audit logs.
  • Verify OpenSCAP scans.
  • Verify file integrity.
